bugs.title: Duplicate order_in_day values in itinerary
bugs.description: Multiple itinerary items within the same day share identical order_in_day values. For example, Day 3 has two items both with orderInDay=2 (Explore Park Guell and Picasso Museum Visit), and Day 4 has two items with orderInDay=1 (Historic Breakfast at Granja Viader and Gothic Quarter History Walk). This could cause rendering issues or ambiguous ordering in the frontend.
bugs.expected: Each item within a day should have a unique order_in_day value
bugs.actual: Day 3: two items with orderInDay=2; Day 4: two items with orderInDay=1; Day 5: two items with orderInDay=1

bugs.title: AI response contains duplicated content in fullContent
bugs.description: When asked about hotel check-in time, the AI's fullContent field contained what appears to be two separate responses concatenated together. The first part discusses check-in time and itinerary updates, then the second part repeats the check-in answer and adds restaurant recommendations. This suggests the tool loop may be generating duplicate response text when multiple tool calls are made in sequence.
bugs.expected: A single coherent response in fullContent
bugs.actual: The fullContent field contained ~2x the expected text with the check-in answer repeated and additional content appended. Total response was over 1000 words for a simple check-in time question.
bugs.api_command: SendMessage with content 'What time is hotel check-in?'

ux_issues.description: The AI proactively renamed the trip from 'Barcelona Getaway' to 'Barcelona Getaway: Elena's Summer Escape' without being asked. While well-intentioned, users like Priya who are detail-oriented may find unsolicited changes to trip metadata annoying.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should ask before renaming trips or only rename when explicitly asked.

ux_issues.description: When asked a simple factual question ('What time is hotel check-in?'), the AI also proactively created multiple itinerary items and updated trip dates. While this is helpful in some contexts, it feels overwhelming for a simple question. The AI should differentiate between planning requests and information queries.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider adding heuristics to detect when the user is asking a factual question vs requesting planning assistance, and avoid proactive itinerary creation for simple information queries.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI recommended El Xampanyet (a bar known for cava/sparkling wine) in the itinerary tapas crawl suggestions despite the persona stating she does not drink much alcohol. The tapas crawl in El Born also mentions 'hop between bars'.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Priya's persona states 'You do not drink much alcohol'. The AI's Day 2 evening includes a tapas crawl mentioning bars, and Day 4 includes El Xampanyet which is primarily a wine/cava bar.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: The AI's fullContent for the hotel check-in question contained duplicated content, repeating the check-in time answer and appending additional planning content. This appears to be a response concatenation issue in the tool loop.
ai_behavior_issues.context: SendMessage with 'What time is hotel check-in?' produced a fullContent that was roughly double the expected length with repeated information.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The booking ingestion pipeline is excellent. All three bookings (flight, hotel, activity) were parsed accurately with correct extraction of confirmation codes, terminals, dates, times, and detailed fields. The AI correctly answered specific questions about booking data (Terminal 4 at JFK, 3:00 PM check-in, June 17 at 10:00 AM Sagrada Familia) using the ingested data. ExtractBookingField returned rich structured data for all three booking types. The recommend_booking tool triggered appropriately for bookable day trips and correctly did NOT trigger for restaurant recommendations. FTC affiliate disclosure was included in the booking recommendation response. The itinerary intelligently incorporated bookings at correct positions. Companion mode responses were concise and practical. Minor issues: duplicate order_in_day values in itinerary items, an overly verbose response with duplicated content for the check-in question, and bar/alcohol recommendations despite the persona's stated preference.
